title: Authenticated Encryption with AES-GCM · Language Lens: Crypto Go Gcm Record Format

Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define magic, version, suite, key ID, nonce, ciphertext, tag, lengths, and parsing limits before calling Open. A defensible solution applies Crypto Go Gcm Record Format, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned AES-GCM record envelope.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	key := make([]byte, 16)
	key[0] = 5
	block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	aead, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	nonce := make([]byte, aead.NonceSize())
	nonce[len(nonce)-1] = 5
	aad := []byte("v1|tenant-a|record-5")
	ciphertext := aead.Seal(nil, nonce, []byte("private-evidence"), aad)
	plaintext, openErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, aad)
	tampered := append([]byte(nil), ciphertext...)
	tampered[len(tampered)-1] ^= 1
	_, tamperErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, tampered, aad)
	fmt.Printf("opened=%t plaintext=%s tamper_rejected=%t\n", openErr == nil, plaintext, tamperErr != nil)
}
// Worked focus: crypto-go-gcm-record-format — Define magic, version, suite, key ID, nonce, ciphertext, tag, lengths, and parsing limits before calling Open.
